item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The issue is that Catholicism denies same-sex couples are a thing and refuses to allow them to marry, not that the witnesses are specific.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Snip, n/m

item.title: Re: Submerging
item.content:encoded: Yep, that's how some communities go about baptizing, if not all out plunging people in a body of water. There have even been some drownings in particularly zealous groups.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I hate this. Great job.

Easily some of the most disturbing writing I’ve ever seen in an article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really enjoyed the extensive description of the events, but something that really stuck out to me was…why specifically state that the affected children tend to join anti-capitalist groups? It just seems really out of place with the rest of the article.

item.title: Submerging
item.content:encoded:
"Blue-Level events proceed identically, despite the presence of a third godparent, until the child is submerged into the water"

Is it what they do in US? Cause I've seen Catholic babtisms and there is no submerging, just pouring water on the head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know it's necroposting, however just for commenting on the "groomsman" issue - actually, afaik, all is required for Catholic wedding are two witnesses of whatever gender. Also, they are not "assigned" to bride or the groom. They are witnesses to confirm what happened, in case there are any doubts whether the marriage happened.

Traditionally it's male and female and probably it is the groom who chooses the male and bride who chooses the female and there are related traidtional duties invloved (like maid of honor helping the bride and best man bringing the rings and then helping the groom). But this is not required during the marriage itself.

The text suggests that it is going on mostly in US, so I guess SCP follows local traditions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, that's just scary. It would be the perfect psychological thriller, if approached from the right perspective.
I know 500 isn’t the milestone it used to be, but this is my first one. Yay 500 upvotes!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with you 100% my dude. I have the exact same feelings for this thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personally, I dislike this SCP, mainly because it feels like its gorey for the sake of being gorey. It's just some dude that shows up, gets people to mutilate one another in various detailed ways, then leaves and have practically all the same side effects with people becoming sterile, or children becoming violent, ect. It's like those horror movies that show all this blood and gore and think that those things alone are scary. This doesn't feel scary, nor really disgusting either. Its throwing all these gross concepts around and trying to get them to stick. It feels like it's trying to hard to be scary and thus fails as a result.
I'm not saying that SCPs shouldn't have blood or gore, plenty already do, but they impliment them in a better more subtle and disturbing way than this one. This one just hits you over the head constantly with whatever it can find. I'm sorry but I just cant enjoy this one. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am SUCH a huge fan of the idea that this is some Eldritch cicada monster desperate to engage with human social gatherings out of loneliness, if only because of how hilariously fucked up that is. Either way, this is really well-written, and the consequences for affected families made me cringe with sympathy.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some Helpful Advice: Insulting the President is bound to lead to stupid political arguments that go on for way to long. K bye! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just wanted to note that Tibicen Linnei is now called Neotibicen Linnei

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I can understand why some people find this article enjoyable, it doesn't work for me at all. It feels like it goes overboard with the amount of gross and disgusting things, which makes them ineffective. The article comes off as relying on the shock factor way too much.

After checking some of the comments, I understood that there is probably another layer to it and I didn't get some of the implications, but this fact doesn't really change my opinion. The piece just focused too much on the gruesome details to convey that another layer properly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy shit, can't believe I forgot to upvote this!

The day I discovered the wiki, I went on a skip-reading binge including SCP-1981, this one, and SCP-1875. Only three to actually give me nightmares.

What I like the most about this skip is how these horrifying things happen to people's bodies and everyone is acting like it's completely normal. Also hit home because I have a very strong bond with my family so it's incredibly creepy to think this creature could attack me through that.

+1

item.title: Re: 400 yay
item.content:encoded: Wahoo! *throws confetti*

item.title: 400 yay
item.content:encoded: I never made a post like this, but it's cool to see that this little guy finally reached 400. My highest rated and, I think, my second scp I wrote. This is the one I may not be the most proud of, but there's a part of me that's always happy to see how much people love a horror story I wrote about a weird picture of my dad I found.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The unreal details of the happenings, its bizarreness, and how all involved are unaware really mixes well into an atmosphere of impossibly creepy benign ceremony that nearly makes me wonder if anything odd was going on at my own drowning baptism. Also as a filthy atheist/socialist it's an amusing thought that an SCP would have both a stake and role in destroying Catholic families.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If it always inserts itself as a groomsman, does that mean it's unable to manifest at weddings with two wives and no groom?

Edit: I later remembered that Catholocism is a bigoted ideology. Never mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think, due to their interest in, you know, keeping up norm and whatnot, that a skip that threatened the bond of the nuclear family would be terrifying to them.

While I don't dispute the truth of this, the fact that this creates extremely messed up events and results in extreme levels of sterility, psychopathy, violence, and death is higher up on the consideration level than the threat to the nuclear family. At least, with this particular SCP.

Anyways, the black level events are one of the most viscerally disturbing things I've read on this site. Congrats.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn. That was hardcore. Where in the world did you get the idea for this? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Late response, but:

Of course the Foundation is conservative. They are an organization that forcibly detains anomalous phenomenon to maintain a veil of normalcy and uphold the status quo. They draft prison labor to perform menial or otherwise dangerous tasks. Their very goddamn motto ("Secure. Contain. Protect.") could double as the motto for Homeland Security or any other border patrol (and isolationism is a fundamentally conservative concept).

Now, you can argue that they've got great reasons to be so conservative; you can also argue that in recent years, writers have given them way more complexity (one thing I personally like to do is portray them as evolving a more nuanced, less reactionary approach to anomalies over time; that's just my headcanon, though), but you can't argue that the core premise behind the Foundation isn't conservative.

That doesn't mean they all vote Republican or whatever. I imagine most of the Foundation hates, say, Trump — because most of the Foundation actually has a brain. But if the Overseer Council isn't conservative as fuck, then the word 'conservative' has officially ceased to have any meaning at all.

(Sorry about the rant, this isn't meant to be antagonistic/hostile toward you; this is just something that seems so obvious to me that it's super-weird when anyone thinks otherwise)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cousin Johnny always makes family events fun.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All Christianity is Judeo. The Christ was a Jew, we read their holy books in our churches. Though, in the book of Exodus, the God of Israel used locusts to torment the Egyptians.

It was fun to read through this and determine if Cousin Johnny is supposed to be God, whom we are supposed to eat, or the devil tricking people into sacrilege. Judging by the murdering, it's option b.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree, completely, and want to add that the excessive focus on making this clearly anti-conservative resulted in a lot of sections becoming a little off.

Sincerely, I doubt the Foundation is this conservative as an organization, to the point of caring if they are anti-capitalism or not. They consistently keep themselves absent from politics and such.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The events described seemed comedic in their absurdity and intent to gross me out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel this, but I’m def not gonna change it at this point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"It is theorized that nearly a third of Catholic and Anglican D-Class were involved in a Black-Level event."

There's over 1 billion Catholics in the world, entire family trees are being wiped out, potentially dozens of people are being killed in killing sprees each time a cicada chirps near an affected individual, 1/3 of D-Class are cicada cursed, and this SCP is simply too powerful.

This might have earned an upvote from me, but the author went overboard on the effects, frequency, and affected groups. Maybe narrow the groups and frequency down a bit (location? Time frame- maybe this happens in a x-month window every x years, or something like that?), and remove or tone down some of the effects, or the amount of individuals affected/involved in the events. Right now, nuking a family and turning them into very obvious maniacs just seems like too much- especially since this can apparently happen to literally any Catholic or Anglican family at random.

Cousin Johnny is very cool, and I was on board with this until everything started getting way out of hand (IMO).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What kind of comedies are you watching?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now maybe I'm missing something, but this just read like a bad, raunchy comedy, with gross-out moment after gross-out moment but no rhyme or reason to anything.

Plus "children in anguish" is kind of a cheap trope.

item.title: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Edit: Edit made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show
- Hide

To link to this skip's sequel, I propose the following text be added to the bottom of the article below the description of the black incidents:

For further research regarding related phenomena, please see the database entry for SCP-3004.

item.title: Cicada people
item.content:encoded: Interesting,

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like to think the Foundation is comically conservative.
Happy +200, cousin johnny. This is a good example of old school creepy pasta done right. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The only thing I didn't like is that "anti-capitalist movements" are somehow equal to "violence" and "terrorism". Everything else is splendid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
could be like a cicada that somehow became humanish and is trying to behave in human soceity
but it hung out around a Catholic church and only can remember that, and mixes it with how it normally behaves

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*takes a deep breath* Phew. This one was hard to digest. In a good way.
The re-occuring themes, as many others have noted before me, really made this stand out from random, morbid horror non-sense. The feeling that there is something behind all this makes it all the more terrifying. Well done sir, well done. I won't sleep tonight.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For question 1, I assumed the Foundation got the knowledge by observing Black events. Maybe it came from the bodycam of an MTF member after they tried to stop the ritual and instead got pulled into it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy shit, this is creepy.

I think some of the most scary SCP's are the ones that are horrifying, yet we have no idea what the hell they are.

+2, if I could. +1, since I can't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is theorized that nearly a third of Catholic and Anglican D-Class were involved in a Black-Level event.

Good god, how has nobody commented on this. It's casually stuck in there at the end, but those little words drive home how widespread this thing us. Ugh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this comment made my eon. especially fitting that your profile pic is "Yes Man" from Fallout New Vegas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really, really enjoying the cicadas "Cousin Johnny" aspect of this SCP. The nonsensical talking, a fake family member appearing, inserting itself into important ceremonies and causing anomalous events. I am not as big a fan of how far the effects go though. Feels like too much happening in my opinion. The black events sound like they would literally destroy entire family trees. Do the Class-A amnestics actually lessen the effects or just keep the person from remembering the events directly?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would like it known that I showed my mother this SCP, because she was curious about how I used the picture of my father. She, uh, hasn't said much besides calling me a weirdo and sitting stunned, so hey I think it worked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough. I guess, for me, it's just too much ick. Maybe i'd like it better if the Blue and White level events weren't as intense/gross, but seeing as this is sitting at +102 i guess my personal preference doesn't matter too much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, we can do a lot to alleviate its effects. Which are noted in the containment procedures. Even black events when found, a long as those affected are watched, we can minimize the grossness. It's just, well, hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well-written, but i'm just not a fan of the relentlessly awful SCPs that we can do nothing about.

item.title: Re: Leaving aside items already discussed . . .
item.content:encoded: Perhaps just a photo lower down in the article of a cicada, with a descriptive caption. For some reason pictures of bugs can increase the tension in the reader. Guess some folds don't like 'em too much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea that it just wants to go to parties and stuff :D Upvoting

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm so happy I finally got credit for Cater Duty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
“Cater Duty”

Beautiful.

Overall, holy crap. Yeah there's a lot of gore and gross stuff here, but the creepy cicada theme and just the idea of some weird insect being infesting and corrupting family gatherings is just… eugh. This is one I'm going to remember for sure. And have nightmares about. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh god. This was horrifying. And amazing. Upvoted hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's always fun when you realize your mouth has been slowly opening in horror over the course of an article. I enjoyed the main thrust and particulars of this article, but there were two big confusing points that stuck out for me.
1.) Knowledge of the SCP. We have detailed knowledge of their anatomy, but there are no references made to capture, containment, death of subjects, etc. It actually makes it sound like they cannot be caught, in which case this knowledge is impossible. Maybe flesh this out in the Containment Procedures? "Instances neutralized through the use of DDT and fire before completing Blue-level events maintain a physical form that can be autopsied." What happens when the events are disrupted?

2.) Physical damage during event. Each class of events has horrific amounts of physical violence being done, but the focus is on the results down the line. Do these wounds heal at the conclusion of the event? It seems like this would be a way bigger and harder to conceal anomalous event (bride and groom missing teeth, f'rinstance) than eventual data clusters. The marriage probably would end in divorce if they woke up after the wedding and someone had pulled their teeth! Curious as to how this could be handled. Maybe if the event is interrupted the wounds don't heal, but otherwise they leave physically fit?

Really enjoyed this otherwise, look forward to seeing more.

item.title: Re: Leaving aside items already discussed . . .
item.content:encoded: I think just Tibicen linnei, maybe with a line or two from wikipedia. Maybe a link to the image of the cicada too, thing is quite a sight and elevated my enjoyment of the piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hadn't even considered option 2! I love it! Y'know, in a gives me chills kind of way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They become fantastic at murdering their parents.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's nothing Judeo- about this. It draws pretty straightforwardly on Christian mythology.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From a religious standpoint I really like how much of this draws on judeo-christian mythology. All the cannibalism-related stuff ties in well to the eucharist (eating body and blood of Christ, and all that). The cicadas were a nice touch.

My only problem was that it relies too much on shock value at some points, but that's more of a personal preference. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something to point out though is that Catholic communities in the United States are actually far less likely to have a large number of nuclear families than Protestant communities. In fact I thought that would be a part of why they were targeted. Catholics tend to form more clan-like extended families that take up large population centers compared to the relatively compact American Protestant families. So this creature like Henry Ford breaks up the clan in favor of smaller less communal families.
